name: sgs/nav-drawer
title: SGS Nav Drawer
description: The mobile off-canvas menu the burger opens. A full-screen native <dialog> modal (top layer — survives a transformed header ancestor). Its content is editable InnerBlocks (menu, logo, CTA); the × close button is fixed chrome the block always renders. Open/close/focus/scroll-lock/ESC are owned by the shared sgs/nav Interactivity store.

dialog.label: Drawer container
dialog._note: Root `<dialog>`. Block-private (containerMirror:false, per the render.php header note — a `<dialog>` root falls outside SGS_Container_Wrapper's $allowed_tags, so this mirrors the wrapper's fill/box capability without calling it). drawerBg resolves via explicit attrMap (name doesn't carry a Colour/Background suffix); border-width/style/color/radius resolve via the skip-serialised native __experimentalBorder support (nativeSupportsPath), not a flat attr. `width` is dual-purpose (edge=left/right -> panel width, edge=top -> panel height via the SAME attribute) so css:height is an honest gap — there is no separate height attribute. The WCAG-computed foreground (drawerFgHex) has no independent client-facing attribute — it is always DERIVED from drawerBg, never set directly — so css:color (text cluster) is deliberately NOT declared here.

body.label: Drawer content
body._note: The `.sgs-nav-drawer__body` flex column that hosts the editable InnerBlocks (menu/logo/CTA). Layer GRID because it is the arrangement layer for this composite (flex-direction:column is a fixed style.css invariant, not an attribute — honest gap on css:flex-direction). drawerAlign maps to css:align-items (left/center/right -> flex-start/center/flex-end); drawerGap/drawerPadding are the responsive object-model box members.

close.label: Close button
close._note: The undeletable x close button, fixed chrome rendered as a sibling of $content (FR-36-6). toggleCloseColour resolves css:color via the default {prefix}{suffix} convention (toggleClose + Colour). The button's :hover opacity fade (style.css:90-92, opacity:0.75) and its :focus-visible outline (style.css:94-97) have NO attribute behind them — an uncontrollable-styling finding, not a manifest gap (no state is declared for this element for that reason).
